name: Neua Pad Kimao (Beef Flambe)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Beef, sliced thinly, diagonally across the grain
3 tbsp Prik ki nu daeng (red birdseye chilis), thinly sliced
3 Bai makrut (kaffir lime leaves), torn, or 1 teaspoon lime zest
recipeInstructions:
This is perhaps one for the adventurous - or the outdoor chef? - for not only is the meal cooked in very hot oil, but it is deliberately flamed at the end! Because of this I recommend you use a wok, not a skillet, at least 16 inches in diameter and 6 inches deep, or a sauté pan, at least 16 inches in diameter and 4 inches deep.
1. Place a wok over very high heat and stir fry the ingredients quickly, stirring or shaking the wok vigorously to prevent the meat from burning.
2. Add 1 cup of warmed Maekhong (rice whiskey) to the wok and tip it to cause it to ignite. After about 30 seconds if it is still burning place the lid on the wok to turn off the flames.
Serving & Storage
Serve with the usual table condiments and white rice.
On a cold night the best "wine" to go with this is whiskey (good sipping whiskey), warmed in the style of sake. On a hot summer day, accompany it with a robust, well-chilled beer, such as Singha or Beer Chang (a Thai product of the Carlsberg breweries that is 9% by volume alcohol!).
